hypothesis: Deploying a swarm of LLM agents with systematically varied model temperatures to explore the same ALP reactor simulation in parallel, with a meta-agent that synthesizes their divergent experimental trajectories in real time, will discover a significantly greater fraction of the total chemical interaction space—including rare effects requiring extended persistence—compared to sequential single-agent runs, because higher-temperature agents will escape local minima that trap lower-temperature agents while lower-temperature agents efficiently exploit promising signals detected by their higher-temperature counterparts.
